texto: No, it's okay. It was just … weird. No one has ever called me hot before.”“Really?” Trace frowned. “Well, that changes right now.” He ceased walking, stopping in the dead center of the pathway and reached for my hands. “Jade Cannon, you are totally hot!” Trace announced loudly, and people nearby stopped to stare at us after his outburst. I couldn't help but laugh.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Chelsea Lynn Charters que provoca temas ligados a amor. Ao questionar “No, it's okay. It was just … weird. No one has ever called me hot before.”“Really?” Trace frowned…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, contemporary-romance, high-school, com foco em mudanca e high-school.

frase_original: No, it's okay. It was just … weird. No one has ever called me hot before.”“Really?” Trace frowned. “Well, that changes right now.” He ceased walking, stopping in the dead center of the pathway and reached for my hands. “Jade Cannon, you are totally hot!” Trace announced loudly, and people nearby stopped to stare at us after his outburst.
